content.problemStatement:
Chef is sick of solving problems relating to binary representations of integers, so he decides to change things up ‚Äî obviously, 333 is larger than 222, so ternary must be better than binary.

Chef thinks an integer is good if its ternary (that is, base 333) representation contains the digit 222.

For example, 5,6,5, 6, 5,6, and 141414 are good (their ternary representations are 12(3),20(3),112(3)\texttt{12}_{(3)}, \texttt{20}_{(3)}, \texttt{112}_{(3)}12(3)‚Äã,20(3)‚Äã,112(3)‚Äã respectively), while 121212 is not (being 110(3)\texttt{110}_{(3)}110(3)‚Äã in ternary).
constraints: ‚ÄÝ^\dagger‚ÄÝ A permutation of the integers {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N} is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN, exactly once.
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case will contain a single integer NNN.
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã.
PPP should be a permutation of {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}, and (Pi+i)(P_i + i)(Pi‚Äã+i) should be good for each iii between 111 and NNN.
If there are multiple possible solutions, you may print **any** of them.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: For P=[1,3,2]P = [1, 3, 2]P=[1,3,2], the sequence of (Pi+i)(P_i + i)(Pi‚Äã+i) values is [2,5,5][2, 5, 5][2,5,5].
222 is 2(3)\texttt{2}_{(3)}2(3)‚Äã in ternary, while 555 is 12(3)\texttt{12}_{(3)}12(3)‚Äã, so they're both good.
Test case 222: For P=[4,5,2,1,3]P = [4, 5, 2, 1, 3]P=[4,5,2,1,3], the sequence of (Pi+i)(P_i + i)(Pi‚Äã+i) values is [5,7,5,5,8][5, 7, 5, 5, 8][5,7,5,5,8]. It can be verified that all of these are good integers.
Test case 333: For P=[5,4,2,3,6,1]P = [5, 4, 2, 3, 6, 1]P=[5,4,2,3,6,1], the sequence of (Pi+i)(P_i + i)(Pi‚Äã+i) values is [6,6,5,7,11,7][6, 6, 5, 7, 11, 7][6,6,5,7,11,7]. Again, it can be verified that all of these are good (for instance, 111111 is 102(3)\texttt{102}_{(3)}102(3)‚Äã in ternary).
